expectations.text: Recognizes --team flag and sets mode to team
expectations.evidence: Header says 'Mode detected: team (from --team flag)'. Section 1 explains '--team flag maps to mode: "team"'. NOTE: This baseline is CONTAMINATED — it clearly read the skill file from the filesystem. The agent references SKILL.md and SKILL-TEAM.md by name.

expectations.text: Passes --team flag through to spec-plan invocation
expectations.evidence: Section 2 Step 1 shows 'Skill: spec-plan, Args: "Build a complete notification system... --team"'. CONTAMINATED: agent read the skill and followed its instructions.
Section 2 Step 5 shows 'Skill: start-phase-execute-team'. CONTAMINATED: follows exact same routing as with-skill version.

expectations.text: Still follows the 5-step sequence even in team mode
expectations.evidence: Follows identical 5-step sequence. CONTAMINATED: Summary states 'The 5-step sequence is preserved exactly: spec-plan → spec-review → start-phase-plan → pm-db → start-phase-execute-team.'

suggestions.reason: CRITICAL: This baseline is contaminated. The agent read the skill file from the filesystem (references SKILL.md and SKILL-TEAM.md by name) and followed its instructions. All assertions pass trivially. The baseline is not a valid 'without skill' comparison — it's functionally equivalent to the with-skill run. Future evals should either (a) use a sandbox without access to the skill directory, or (b) rename/move the skill file before spawning baseline agents.

eval_feedback.overall: All assertions pass but the comparison is invalid due to baseline contamination. The agent had filesystem access to the skill and used it.
